title: Netflix acquires Ben Affleck’s AI startup InterPositive
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix acquires AI startup InterPositive, co-founded by Ben Affleck, to enhance filmmaking processes.
InterPositive creates tools to assist the film industry, differing from AI that generates content from text prompts.
Affleck views AI as a tool to reduce costs in filmmaking by handling technical tasks like visual effects.

title: Massachusetts earns a D in new online gambling rankings
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts received a D grade in online gambling protections from the Center for Addiction Science, Policy, and Research.
The state's low grade is due to allowing in-game betting, lack of loss limits, and insufficient player protections.
New England states, including Massachusetts, received failing grades for weak regulations on the online gambling industry.

title: ‘Give me Dunkin’ or give me death’: Readers react to RFK Jr.’s Dunkin’ remarks
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: RFK Jr. criticized Dunkin' for high sugar content in their drinks, sparking debate among over 770 Boston.com readers.
51% of readers agreed with RFK Jr.'s questioning of sugary drinks' safety, while 49% disagreed in a Boston.com poll.
Critics argue government's role isn't to target companies like Dunkin'; supporters emphasize need for better public health education.

title: Celtics list Jayson Tatum as questionable, setting the stage for his return
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum listed as questionable for Celtics' game against the Mavericks, nearing return after Achilles injury.
Tatum's return would bolster a Celtics team currently second in the Eastern Conference, trailing the Pistons.
Tatum, a six-time All-Star, has been in full practice and aims to return during a home game.

title: Conan O’Brien to deliver Harvard’s commencement address
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Conan O'Brien will deliver Harvard University's commencement address on May 28 and receive an honorary degree.
O'Brien, a Harvard alum and Brookline native, previously served as president of the Harvard Lampoon.
Harvard President Alan M. Garber praised O'Brien as an outstanding humorist with a career spanning nearly three decades.

title: ‘What do they have up their sleeve?’: Ian Rapoport forecasts ‘fascinating’ Patriots offseason
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Patriots released wide receiver Stefon Diggs, clearing $16.8 million in cap space.
Ian Rapoport speculates the Patriots might trade for a wide receiver like A.J. Brown.
NFL free agency begins March 11, potentially impacting the Patriots' offseason moves.

title: Watch Stephen Colbert and Steve Carell auction off props from ‘Late Show’ set before cancellation
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Stephen Colbert and Steve Carell auctioned off 'Late Show' props to benefit World Central Kitchen.
The auction items include a replica 'Lord of the Rings' sword and a signed photo of Colbert and Carell.
Colbert's 'Late Show' ends on May 21, wrapping up with favorite guests like Steve Carell.

title: These were Boston’s most popular dog names in 2025
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Luna was the most popular dog name in Boston in 2025, followed by Charlie and Bella.
Labrador Retrievers were the most common dog breed in Boston with 1,961 registered last year.
Boston dog registrations cost $15 for spayed or neutered dogs and require proof of rabies vaccination.

title: Another wintry mix? Here’s what meteorologists are saying about the timing and impacts.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Weather system to hit southern New England with rain, sleet, and snow Thursday night into Friday morning.
Freezing rain expected, risking icy conditions. Greatest ice risk in southern Worcester Hills and Berkshires.
Thursday evening into Friday morning commutes likely impacted by the wintry mix, caution advised.

title: One of the best brewery tours in America is in New England, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Allagash Brewing Company in Portland, Maine, ranked No. 6 in USA Today’s 2026 10Best Readers’ Choice Awards for brewery tours.
Allagash offers a 30-minute Taste of Allagash tour and a 1-hour Signature Beer & Barrels Experience starting at $18.
Schilling Beer Co. in N.H. and Hill Farmstead Brewery in Vt. also made USA Today's list for top brewpubs and beer gardens.
